title: collomia debilis f. howardii
intro: Collomia debilis f. howardii, also known as Howard's collomia, is a rare, perennial herb of the phlox family (Polemoniaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of collomia debilis f. howardii include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
